clues: This Oklahoma born actress, now 63, made her movie debut in 1992's Basic Instinct.
She went on to appear in some of the defining movies of the decade, including The Firm (1993), Reality Bites (1994), and Sliding Doors (1998).
She played Mormon wife Barbara Henrickson on the HBO show Big Love from 2006 - 2010.
This New Jersey native guest starred in a string of 1970s TV shows, including The Rockford Files, Starsky & Hutch, and The Love Boat.
The actress, now 72, is best remembered for her role in Three's Company.
She joined the show for its final three seasons playing sensible nurse Terri Alden from 1981 to 1984.
This actress, now age 66, had her breakout role as Private Vasquez in the 1986 blockbuster Aliens, directed by James Cameron.
She subsequently starred as the ruthless vampire Diamondback in Kathryn Bigelow's 1987 movie Near Dark.
The Los Angeles born star also appeared as John Connor's foster mom in James Cameron's Terminator 2: Judgement Day (1991).
This small-screen star, now age 78, had minor roles on classic 1990s sitcoms, including Seinfeld and Roseanne.
In 2018, she scored a Daytime Emmy Award for her guest appearance on the soap opera The Bay.
The star's Emmy was infamously revoked after it was discovered that her entry included clips from multiple episodes, instead of one.
This Brooklyn born actress was raised on Long Island in a large Catholic family.
She became a familiar on-screen face in the 1990s, appearing in hit movies Independence Day and The Devil's Own.
The star played Blair Waldorf's stylish, sharp-tongued mother, Eleanor in the teen drama Gossip Girl, which ran from 2007 to 2012.
